name: Use of eval()
description: eval() executes arbitrary code from a string — a direct path to code injection.
fixHint: Replace eval() with JSON.parse() for data or a dedicated parser.

name: Use of new Function()
description: new Function() constructs and executes code at runtime — equivalent risk to eval().
fixHint: Use a static function or a safe expression evaluator library.

name: Use of dangerouslySetInnerHTML
description: dangerouslySetInnerHTML bypasses React's XSS protection and directly injects HTML.
fixHint: Sanitize with DOMPurify before setting HTML, or use standard JSX rendering.

name: child_process exec with template literal
description: Executing shell commands built from template literals allows command injection.
fixHint: Use execFile() or spawn() with an explicit argument array instead of a shell string.

name: require() with non-literal argument
description: Dynamic require() paths can lead to path traversal or unintended module loading.
fixHint: Use a static string literal in require(). Whitelist allowed modules if dynamic loading is needed.

name: Use of document.write()
description: document.write() can inject arbitrary HTML and is a vector for XSS.
